class PriorityQueue
  def initialize
    @heap = []
  end

  def push(value)
    @heap << value
    heapify_up(@heap.length - 1)
  end

  def pop
    return nil if @heap.empty?
    min = @heap[0]
    @heap[0] = @heap.pop
    heapify_down(0)
    min
  end

  private

  def heapify_up(index)
    while index > 0
      parent = (index - 1) / 2
      if @heap[parent][0] <= @heap[index][0]
        break
      end
      @heap[index], @heap[parent] = @heap[parent], @heap[index]
      index = parent
    end
  end

  def heapify_down(index)
    length = @heap.length
    while index < length
      left = 2 * index + 1
      right = 2 * index + 2
      smallest = index
      if left < length && @heap[left][0] < @heap[smallest][0]
        smallest = left
      end
      if right < length && @heap[right][0] < @heap[smallest][0]
        smallest = right
      end
      return if smallest == index
      @heap[index], @heap[smallest] = @heap[smallest], @heap[index]
      index = smallest
    end
  end
end

def solve(grid, k)
  m = grid.length
  n = grid[0].length

  teleportTo = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { [] } }
  for i in 0...m
    for j in 0...n
      val = grid[i][j]
      for x in 0...m
        for y in 0...n
          if grid[x][y] <= val
            teleportTo[i][j] << [x, y]
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  dist = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Array.new(k+1, Float::INFINITY) } }
  dist[0][0][0] = 0

  pq = PriorityQueue.new
  pq.push([0, 0, 0, 0])

  while !pq.empty?
    cost, i, j, t = pq.pop
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    if cost > dist[i][j][t]
      next
    end

    # Regular moves
    if j + 1 < n
      new_cost = cost + grid[i][j+1]
      if new_cost < dist[i][j+1][t]
        dist[i][j+1][t] = new_cost
        pq.push([new_cost, i, j+1, t])
      end
    end
    if i + 1 < m
      new_cost = cost + grid[i+1][j]
      if new_cost < dist[i+1][j][t]
        dist[i+1][j][t] = new_cost
        pq.push([new_cost, i+1, j, t])
      end
    end

    # Teleportation
    if t < k
      teleportTo[i][j].each do |(x, y)|
        new_t = t + 1
        if cost < dist[x][y][new_t]
          dist[x][y][new_t] = cost
          pq.push([cost, x, y, new_t])
        end

    end
  end

def solve(grid, k)
  m = grid.length
  n = grid[0].length

  teleportTo = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { [] } }
  for i in 0...m
    for j in 0...n
      val = grid[i][j]
      for x in 0...m
        for y in 0...n
          if grid[x][y] <= val
            teleportTo[i][j] << [x, y]
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  dist = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Array.new(k+1, Float::INFINITY) } }
  dist[0][0][0] = 0

  pq = PriorityQueue.new
  pq.push([0, 0, 0, 0])

  while !pq.empty?
    cost, i, j, t = pq.pop
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    if cost > dist[i][j][t]
      next
    end

    # Regular moves
    if j + 1 < n
      new_cost = cost + grid[i][j+1]
      if new_cost < dist[i][j+1][t]
        dist[i][j+1][t] = new_cost
        pq.push([new_cost, i, j+1, t])
      end
    end
    if i + 1 < m
      new_cost = cost + grid[i+1][j]
      if new_cost < dist[i+1][j][t]
        dist[i+1][j][t] = new_cost
        pq.push([new_cost, i+1, j, t])
      end
    end

    # Teleportation
    if t < k
      teleportTo[i][j].each do |(x, y)|
        new_t = t + 1
        if cost < dist[x][y][new_t]
          dist[x][y][new_t] = cost
          pq.push([cost, x, y, new_t])
        end
      end
    end
  end

  -1
end

def solve(grid, k)
  m = grid.length
  n = grid[0].length

  teleportTo = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { [] } }

    for j in 0...n
      for x in 0...m
        for y in 0...n

            teleportTo[i][j] << [x, y]
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  dist = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Array.new(k+1, Float::INFINITY) } }
  dist[0][0][0] = 0

  class PriorityQueue
    attr_reader :heap
    def initialize
      @heap = []
    end

    def push(value)
      @heap << value
      heapify_up(@heap.length - 1)
    end

    def pop
      return nil if @heap.empty?
      min = @heap[0]
      @heap[0] = @heap.pop
      heapify_down(0)
      min
    end

    def heapify_up(index)
      while index > 0
        parent_index = (index - 1) / 2
        if @heap[parent_index][0] <= @heap[index][0]
          break
        end
        @heap[index], @heap[parent_index] = @heap[parent_index], @heap[index]
        index = parent_index
      end
    end

    def heapify_down(index)
      length = @heap.length
      while index < length
        left = 2 * index + 1
        right = 2 * index + 2
        smallest = index
        if left < length && @heap[left][0] < @heap[smallest][0]
          smallest = left
        end
        if right < length && @heap[right][0] < @heap[smallest][0]
          smallest = right
        end
        if smallest != index
          @heap[index], @heap[smallest] = @heap[smallest], @heap[index]
          index = smallest
        else
          break
        end
      end
    end

    def empty?
      @heap.empty?
    end
  end

  pq = PriorityQueue.new
  pq.push([0, 0, 0, 0])

  while !pq.empty?
    cost, i, j, t = pq.pop
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    if cost > dist[i][j][t]
      next
    end

    if j + 1 < n
      new_cost = cost + grid[i][j+1]
      if new_cost < dist[i][j+1][t]
        dist[i][j+1][t] = new_cost
        pq.push([new_cost, i, j+1, t])
      end
    end

    if i + 1 < m
      new_cost = cost + grid[i+1][j]
      if new_cost < dist[i+1][j][t]
        dist[i+1][j][t] = new_cost
        pq.push([new_cost, i+1, j, t])
      end
    end

    if t < k
      teleportTo[i][j].each do |x, y|
        new_t = t + 1
        if cost < dist[x][y][new_t]
          dist[x][y][new_t] = cost

        end

    end

def solve(grid, k)
  m = grid.length
  n = grid[0].length

  # Precompute teleportTo for each cell
  teleportTo = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { [] } }

  for i in 0...m
    for j in 0...n
      val = grid[i][j]
      for x in 0...m
        for y in 0...n
          if grid[x][y] <= val
            teleportTo[i][j] << [x, y]
          end
        end
      end
    end
  end

  # Initialize distance array
  dist = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n) { Array.new(k+1, Float::INFINITY) } }
  dist[0][0][0] = 0

  # Priority queue
  class PriorityQueue
    attr_accessor :heap

    def initialize
      @heap = []
    end

    def push(value)
      @heap << value
      heapify_up(@heap.length - 1)
    end

    def pop
      if @heap.empty?
        return nil
      end
      min = @heap[0]
      @heap[0] = @heap.pop
      heapify_down(0)
      min
    end

    def heapify_up(index)
      while index > 0
        parent_index = (index - 1) / 2
        if @heap[parent_index][0] <= @heap[index][0]
          break
        end
        @heap[index], @heap[parent_index] = @heap[parent_index], @heap[index]
        index = parent_index
      end
    end

    def heapify_down(index)
      length = @heap.length
      while index < length
        left = 2 * index + 1
        right = 2 * index + 2
        smallest = index
        if left < length && @heap[left][0] < @heap[smallest][0]
          smallest = left
        end
        if right < length && @heap[right][0] < @heap[smallest][0]
          smallest = right
        end
        if smallest != index
          @heap[index], @heap[smallest] = @heap[smallest], @heap[index]
          index = smallest
        else
          break
        end
      end
    end

    def empty?
      @heap.empty?
    end
  end

  pq = PriorityQueue.new
  pq.push([0, 0, 0, 0])

  while !pq.empty?
    cost, i, j, t = pq.pop
    if i == m-1 && j == n-1
      return cost
    end
    if cost > dist[i][j][t]
      next
    end

    # Regular moves: right and down
    # Right
    if j + 1 < n
      new_cost = cost + grid[i][j+1]
      if new_cost < dist[i][j+1][t]
        dist[i][j+1][t] = new_cost
        pq.push([new_cost, i, j+1, t])
      end
    end
    # Down
    if i + 1 < m
      new_cost = cost + grid[i+1][j]
      if new_cost < dist[i+1][j][t]
        dist[i+1][j][t] = new_cost
        pq.push([new_cost, i+1, j, t])
      end
    end

    # Teleportation
    if t < k
      teleportTo[i][j].each do |x, y|
        new_t = t + 1
        if cost < dist[x][y][new_t]
          dist[x][y][new_t] = cost
          pq.push([cost, x, y, new_t])
        end
      end
    end
  end

  return -1
end
